attacking_style: Pure bunch sprint finisher who does not attack but positions for final 200m acceleration. Excels on flat, technical circuit finishes where maneuverability matters more than raw power.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on structured leadout train with experienced pilot (Richeze in 2019). Team controls echelons and pace in crosswinds. When leadout fails or is lost in chaos, positioning deteriorates significantly.
terrain_preference: Dead-flat finishes and technical circuit sprints (Madrid). Completely exposed on any gradient or lumpy profiles—dropped before 20km on hilly circuits and struggles with even slight uphill finish gradients.
vulnerability: Cannot handle any climbing or gradient in finale. Prone to mechanical issues and positioning errors inside final 3km. Lost team confidence by 2023, with squad riding for other sprinters instead.
key_patterns: Dropped or distanced on any uphill gradient finish, even slight inclines (3 observations)
Positioning errors or mechanical issues inside final 3km lose contention (3 observations)
Excels on technical circuit finishes requiring maneuverability over pure power (2 observations)
Benefits from crosswind/echelon scenarios when team controls pace (2 observations)
Demonstrated tactical intelligence with intermediate sprint management for points jersey (2 observations)
Lost team leadout protection correlates directly with poor results (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: By 2023 lost team leadership role (Quick-Step rode for Ballerini). Positioning errors and crashes increased. Last confirmed win was March 2022.
